calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2024 Vacated

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT IPV ASSOCIATES, LLC, GRANT-JACKSON, LLC, SONA FASHIONS, INC. DBA THE ULTIMATE SOUVENIR SHOP MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant's motion to strike the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is denied as to plaintiff's "tester" allegations. FAC, pars. 10 (erroneously cited as 13), 25, 27, 29, 30, 31. As noted in connection with the demurrer, plaintiff adequately alleges a bona fide intent, sufficient to support Unruh Act standing; plaintiff's mixed motivations are not "irrelevant, false, or improper" allegations to be stricken under CCP 436(a).

The motion to strike is granted with leave to amend as to the injunctive relief allegations. FAC, pars. 9, prayer, pars. 1 - 3. Plaintiff must present a request for "preventive relief." Civil Code sec. 52(c)(3) provides that an Unruh Act plaintiff can seek "[a] request for preventive relief, including an application for a permanent or temporary injunction." Civil Code sec. 3368 states that "Preventive relief is given by prohibiting a party from doing that which ought not to be done." The scheme therefore contemplates prohibitory-rather than mandatory-injunctive relief. See Balasubramanian v. San Diego Community College Dist. (2000) 80 Cal.App.4th 977, 988 ["We must construe identical words in different parts of the same act or in different statutes relating to the same subject matter as having the same meaning"]. Plaintiff's FAC improperly seeks mandatory relief. "As a general rule, we think we may say that when the injunction merely grants preventive relief it is prohibitive, but when it directly or indirectly grants affirmative relief it is mandatory." Ohaver v. Fenech (1928) 206 Cal. 118, 122.) "[T]he general rule is that an injunction is prohibitory if it requires a person to refrain from a particular act and mandatory if it compels performance of an affirmative act that changes the position of the parties." Davenport v. Blue Cross of California (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 435, 446.) See generally, Daly v. San Bernardino County Bd. of Supervisors (2021) 11 Cal.5th 1030, 1035

Civil Code sec. 52(g) notes: "This section does not require any construction, alteration, repair, structural or otherwise, or modification of any sort whatsoever, beyond that construction, alteration, repair, or modification that is otherwise required by other provisions of law." Plaintiff seems to argue that Thurston v. Midvale (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 634, 651-652 allows a mandatory injunction based on the FAC's allegations. But the case is not useful here, as it did not address the definition of "preventive relief" and rather focuses on Unruh Act standing.

Defense counsel is cautioned regarding the citation to superior court orders, which are not persuasive nor precedential authority and generally must not be cited. Weil & Brown, et al., California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 9:67.7 (Rutter: 2023). The request for judicial notice is granted as to item 1 (AB 181) and denied as to the rest of the items.

A further amended complaint must be filed not later than December 8, 2023.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312. .

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT IPV ASSOCIATES, LLC, GRANT-JACKSON, LLC, SONA FASHIONS, INC. DBA THE ULTIMATE SOUVENIR SHOP DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

Defendants' demurrer to the First Amended Complaint (FAC) is overruled.

Under CCP 452, the court liberally construes the allegations of the complaint. In enforcing the Unruh Civil Rights Act, "courts must consider its broad remedial purpose and overarching goal of deterring discriminatory practices by businesses. [Citations.] [The California Supreme Court has] consistently held that the Act must be construed liberally in order to carry out its purpose. [Citations.] [] In light of its broad preventive and remedial purposes, courts have recognized that '[s]tanding under [the Act] is broad." Thurston v. Omni Hotels Management (2021) 69 Cal.App.5th 299, 306 (Omni) (internal quotations omitted).

Omni explained that a plaintiff must actually suffer discriminatory conduct. "Put another way, the [Unruh Civil Rights Act] is confined to discriminations against recipients of the 'business establishment's ... goods, services or facilities.' [Citation.] [Unruh Civil Rights Act] claims are thus appropriate where the plaintiff was in a relationship with the offending organization similar to that of the customer in the customer-proprietor relationship." Id. (internal quotations omitted). The case requires a "bona fide intent to sign up for or use [defendant's] services." Id. at 307 (italics in original.) "Our standing analysis is limited to the pleadings, in which White unequivocally alleges his intention to use Square's services." White v. Square, Inc. (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1019, 1032 [Unruh Act plaintiff had standing even though he never entered into a transaction with the defendant].

The FAC alleges that "Plaintiff went to the Store on December 14, 2021, with the intention to avail himself of its goods, services, privileges, or advantages ("Benefits") motivated in part to determine if the Defendants comply with the disability access laws." FAC, par. 10. Plaintiff's allegations that he intended to avail himself of defendant's goods is enough for standing, even though he also admits that he was in part motivated to be a "tester". Defendant cites no authority that plaintiff loses standing if he has mixed motivations. The issue here is ultimately factual one, and defendant "may argue in a motion for summary judgment or at trial that [plaintiff] did not actually possess a bona fide intent to sign up for or use its services." White, 7 Cal.5th at 1032.

The court rejects defendant's argument that the FAC is infirm because plaintiff does not plead intentional conduct. Plaintiff seeks damages. FAC, prayer 4. "A plaintiff who establishes a violation of the ADA, therefore, need not prove intentional discrimination in order to obtain damages under section 52." Munson v. Del Taco, Inc. (2009) 46 Cal.4th 661, 665. Under Munson, plaintiff need not allege "intentional" conduct.

ounsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)

BRIAN WHITAKER'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT MOVING PARTY: (MISCALENDARED BY PARTY IN DISCVOERY CALENDAR)

Off Calendar Jul-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff BRIAN WHITAKER's motion for leave to file first amended complaint.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The amended complaint substitutes one set of defendants for another. The old defendants argue that they should be dismissed with prejudice, not just omitted, but they cite no authority for that notion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
